name: Quail Run Golf Course
about: Quail Run Golf Course is a public golf facility in Columbus, Nebraska, offering both traditional play and technology-driven experiences. The venue operates a Van Berg Family Golf Center & TRACKman Lounge, providing simulator technology for golf practice and play. The facility caters to a range of golfers, from serious players to families and groups, with a variety of tee boxes, leagues, and events. Additionally, the site offers comprehensive programs including the FORE! Academy for youth and adults, lessons, private coaching, and footgolf, creating a multi-sport environment centered on golf and recreation.
why_golfers_like_it: Premium TRACKman technology for detailed swing analysis
Dedicated youth and adult coaching programs
Variety of leagues and competitive events for all skill levels
Public access with flexible booking policies

food_and_drink.notes: On-site kitchen and food service available, including complimentary cook-outs for events
No mention of alcohol service; likely BYOB policy

simulator_hardware.notes: High-end radar launch monitor system in the Van Berg Family Golf Center & TRACKman Lounge

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, players should bring their own golf clubs for simulator sessions, though the facility may offer club rentals or have demo equipment available for lessons.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faq.answer: Outside food and drink may be allowed, but the facility has an on-site kitchen that provides food service. Alcohol service is not mentioned, suggesting a BYOB policy.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes on the simulator?
faq.answer: The time for 18 holes can vary, but typical simulator sessions range from 1.5 to 2.5 hours depending on group size and pace of play.

faq.question: Is the facility kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the facility is very kid-friendly, offering the FORE! Academy with youth programs, junior leagues, summer camps, and footgolf for families.
